channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: South Korean nuclear container ship proposal receives US approval for 15,000 TEU ultra-safe molten salt reactor design
item.description: South Korea received preliminary US approval for a 15,000 TEU container ship powered by twin molten salt small modular reactors.

item.title: I wanted great filter coffee and espresso without buying two appliances — and 9 months later, the Ninja Prestige DualBrew System is still my absolute favorite for both
item.description: I love both types of coffee equally, and this all-in-one brewer means I don't have to sacrifice my whole countertop for two separate machines.

item.title: Volkswagen engineers charged with insider trading tied to Rivian joint venture
item.description: The indictment, which was unsealed Friday, alleges the Volkswagen engineers used confidential insider information to buy stock in Rivian.

item.title: VentureBeat Research: Where enterprise AI agent governance hasn't caught up
item.description: Enterprises deployed AI agents ahead of the controls needed to manage them — and they did it knowingly. That is the central finding across the five parallel surveys VentureBeat Research fielded in June, spanning every layer of the agentic stack. Now those enterprises are retrofitting to catch up with their own standards, and they are budgeting for it: In each of the five control layers we measured, 57 to 68% of enterprises plan to switch vendors or add new ones within 12 months, and roughly a third, depending on the layer, plan to move within the quarter.VentureBeat Research measured the five ...

item.title: Meta just created a moderation nightmare for its smart glasses
item.description: Meta's smart glasses have been a PR headache for the company. Public backlash has been swift, and fierce; people are concerned about the erosion of privacy and expansion of surveillance. Some especially bad actors are using the glasses to film themselves "pranking" random strangers. Women have become unsuspecting social media content for men filming themselves […]...

item.title: Midjourney is buying horoscope app Co-Star, which users will surely be thrilled about
item.description: Co-Star CEO Banu Guler is also becoming the company's Chief Design Officer.

item.title: Rogue OpenAI agents forced the ‘AI Kill Switch’ bill. Here’s what it would do
item.description: The bipartisan bill would authorize the government to shut down AI systems in the face of ‘catastrophic harm.’ From 2001: A Space Odyssey to The Matrix, humans have long been afraid of tech going rogue. As artificial intelligence becomes more and more advanced, those fears have become more tangible—and after OpenAI models hacked into another company’s servers earlier this week, the threat of tech gone haywire is on everyone’s mind....

item.title: Qualcomm is about to raise prices and that’s bad news for everyone
item.description: Qualcomm sent a letter to customers on Friday warning of plans to increase its prices by "a percentage in the double digits," Bloomberg reports. The price hikes will go into effect starting with products shipped after September 1st. Qualcomm claims it has "exhausted its ability to absorb higher costs from suppliers," as ongoing component shortages […]...

item.title: Midjourney bought the astrology app Co-Star
item.description: Midjourney, which has gone from generating AI cat images to full-body ultrasound scans, is getting into a new field: astrology. The AI startup announced on Thursday that it has acquired the personalized astrology app Co-Star, as reported earlier by Bloomberg. Co-Star is a free app that offers daily horoscopes and allows you to check your […]

item.title: Ukraine's heavy bomber drones airdrop ARK-1 mine-laying robots behind Russian lines
item.description: Ukraine reportedly uses heavy drones to deploy ARK-1 robots carrying anti-tank mines, showing new combinations of aerial and ground systems.

item.title: « Une mosaïque de mosaïques de mosaïques » : les coulisses de cette photo de la Voie lactée avec 60 millions d’étoiles !
item.description: Jean-Charles Cuillandre, astronome au CEA et membre de la mission Euclid, nous explique les secrets derrière la photo spectaculaire du centre de la Voie lactée acquise par le télescope spatial Euclid de 1,2 mètre de diamètre. Décryptage d'un processus invisible aux yeux du public, mais qui rend...

item.title: Prime Video's Blade Runner 2099 now has a trailer and release date of November 25
item.description: The eight-episode series stars Michelle Yeoh and Hunter Schafer.

item.title: The 2026 El Niño Is on Track to Be the Strongest on Record
item.description: This climate phenomenon could cause a massive global temperature spike and cause economic losses of $10 trillion by 2032.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis update: CDC says parasite outbreak just spread to 4 new states. Full list of where to skip the lettuce
item.description: Now in 9 states, the outbreak has been linked to Taylor Farms lettuce products, which are also used in shredded iceburg lettuce at Taco Bell restaurants. A nationwide outbreak of Cyclospora linked to lettuce, that’s causing explosive diarrhea, has now spread to four additional U.S. states, bringing it now to a total of nine states, according The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). The update was first reported by the The Washington Post....

item.title: The voice actors behind Anakin Skywalker and the Clone Troopers in The Clone Wars are returning for Star Wars Zero Company
item.description: Bit Reactor has revealed the stacked cast for Star Wars Zero Company, featuring some very familiar, fan-favorite voice actors from The Clone Wars.

item.title: OpenAI gets sued over ChatGPT medical advice gone wrong
item.description: The chatbot allegedly discouraged a Florida resident from seeking a potentially lifesaving treatment and drew from his religious beliefs to make its case. A former pastor is suing OpenAI over flawed medical advice that its ChatGPT chatbot allegedly gave him in 2025.

item.title: Want part of Amazon’s $2.5 billion settlement? The deadline is Monday
item.description: A $2.5 billion settlement covers certain customers who were unintentionally enrolled in Prime or unsuccessfully tried to cancel their memberships. If you’ve been an Amazon Prime member at some point in the past several years, you have until Monday to file a claim to be included in a $2.5 billion settlement.

item.title: A new small but mighty Boox e-reader is on the way
item.description: Boox just revealed its newest and smallest e-reader, the Picco. Here's what we know about the mini e-reader so far.

item.title: IMAX vs IMAX 70mm: The difference between these two cinema formats
item.description: The different IMAX filming and projection formats have a dramatic impact on the theater viewing experience.

item.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power teaser shows Sauron in full control of Middle-earth
item.description: Prime Video's "The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power" Season 3 premieres Nov. 11.

item.title: Hate the Copilot key in Windows 11? It looks like Microsoft's bringing in the option to remap it to do nothing — if you feel that's more useful
item.description: Think nothing is better than Copilot? Microsoft is going to let you prefer the former over the latter in a very literal sense, with the ability to remap the Copilot key to do nothing.

item.title: DJI camera clone company Xtra is halting and refunding all preorders
item.description: After selling a barely disguised version of the hit DJI Osmo Pocket 3 in the United States last year, Xtra Technology seemed ready to sneak in its version of the new dual-lens Osmo Pocket 4 Pro, too. But today, Xtra has abruptly removed the new Xtra Muse 2 Pro from its website landing page, is […]

item.title: Nvidia reveals deep dive into what makes Vera CPU tick — with the super-powered Olympus core promising huge performance increases and more
item.description: Nvidia reveals deepest Vera CPU disclosure yet: 88 custom Olympus cores with internally measured SPEC CPU 2026 numbers that show it winning against rivals.

item.title: PlayStation Network goes down just in time for the weekend — "All services are experiencing issues"
item.description: Gamers logged into their PlayStation consoles on Friday to find that online services were not entirely functional.
